response: The internet originated from 1960s U.S. Department of Defense computer networking research, with its precursor ARPANET first demonstrated in 1969, and the 1983 transition to TCP/IP protocols created the formal foundation for the modern internet. Subsequent critical developments included Tim Berners-Lee's 1989 invention of the World Wide Web, the 1993 Mosaic browser that brought the web to general users, the late 1990s dot-com boom and 2001 crash, the mid-2000s rise of social media, 2006's launch of AWS cloud computing, and the 2007 iPhone that popularized ubiquitous mobile internet. Though most dot-com era companies failed in the 2001 crash, survivors including Amazon and Google became some of history's most valuable corporations. Today, the internet connects over 5 billion people worldwide and underpins nearly every part of modern life.
